intelligent-code-enhancer.md.scientific_roast: This command has 41 thinking blocks and 147 template placeholders. That's not "intelligent" enhancement, that's cognitive overload wearing a lab coat. At 1,632 tokens, it costs more to think than to actually enhance code. Complexity score: 6/10 - FAILED simplicity standards. This is like hiring a philosopher to change a light bulb.
intelligent-code-enhancer.md.data_evidence: 41 thinking blocks (σ=21.7 above mean), 442 lines (11% over limit), 5 command chains
intelligent-code-enhancer.md.roi_analysis: Terrible. Costs ~/bin/bash.02 per execution but delivers maybe /bin/bash.005 of value. 75% waste.
intelligent-code-enhancer.md.recommendation: IMMEDIATE REFACTOR: Remove 80% of thinking blocks, cap at 150 lines, eliminate template hell.

java-test-driven-development.md.scientific_roast: TDD command with a complexity score of 7/10? That's like teaching simplicity with a 500-page manual. 27 thinking blocks for TDD methodology - real TDD practitioners think LESS, not more. At 2,063 tokens, this costs more than most junior developer salaries per execution. Martin Fowler would weep.
java-test-driven-development.md.data_evidence: 27 thinking blocks (6 above optimal), 460 lines (53% over recommended), contains premature abstraction patterns
java-test-driven-development.md.roi_analysis: Questionable. High cognitive load (2,063 tokens) for teaching simplicity principles. Ironic inefficiency.
java-test-driven-development.md.recommendation: MAJOR SIMPLIFICATION: Follow your own TDD advice - Red, Green, Refactor this command to <150 lines.

intelligent-refactor-session.md.scientific_roast: THE WORST OFFENDER. Complexity score: 8/10. This "refactor" command has 62 thinking blocks and 534 lines - it needs refactoring more than anything it could possibly refactor. At 2,662 tokens per execution, this command costs more than most entire refactoring sessions. It's like using a bulldozer to arrange flowers. This violates every simplicity principle it claims to enforce.
intelligent-refactor-session.md.data_evidence: 62 thinking blocks (2.3σ above mean), 534 lines (MASSIVE file violation), 90 template placeholders, lowest effectiveness score (1/5)
intelligent-refactor-session.md.roi_analysis: CATASTROPHIC. ~/bin/bash.035 per execution with negative value delivery. This command is a liability.
intelligent-refactor-session.md.recommendation: COMPLETE REWRITE: Start from scratch. Current version is unsalvageable bloatware.

reasoning-wrapper.md.scientific_roast: A meta-command that wraps other commands with reasoning. Complexity: 6/10. It has 35 thinking blocks and chains 11 other commands. This is like adding a thinking hat to someone already wearing 3 thinking hats. At 1,560 tokens, it adds reasoning overhead to everything it touches - making every command more expensive and slower.
reasoning-wrapper.md.data_evidence: 35 thinking blocks, 415 lines, 11 command chains (recursive complexity nightmare), 80 template placeholders
reasoning-wrapper.md.roi_analysis: Meta-inefficiency. Multiplies cost of every command it wraps by ~2.5x while adding minimal value.
reasoning-wrapper.md.recommendation: TARGETED REDUCTION: Eliminate 70% of thinking blocks, reduce command chaining, cap at 200 lines.

generate-thinking-command.md.scientific_roast: Finally, something that doesn't completely violate the laws of software simplicity. Complexity: 3/10 - PASSED! Only 9 thinking blocks, 137 lines. However, it chains 7 commands, so it's still creating complexity multiplication. It's like the one responsible friend in a group of chaos agents.
generate-thinking-command.md.data_evidence: 9 thinking blocks (reasonable), 137 lines (within limits), 7 command chains (concerning)
generate-thinking-command.md.roi_analysis: Acceptable. 540 tokens per execution, delivers proportional value. Best ROI in this disaster collection.
generate-thinking-command.md.recommendation: MINOR OPTIMIZATION: Reduce command chaining by 50%. This one actually shows promise.

overall_roast:
🏆 CONGRATULATIONS! You've achieved a perfect storm of over-engineering! 🏆

STATISTICAL BRUTALIZATION SUMMARY:
- Simplicity Violation Rate: 80% (80% FAILURE RATE)
- Average Complexity: 6.0/10 (20% over acceptable limit)  
- Commands in Violation: 4/5 (Only 1 passes basic standards)
- Standard Deviation: 1.7 (High variance indicates inconsistent quality)
- Total Token Cost per Full Run: 8456 tokens (~/bin/bash.10)

This is not "intelligent" command design. This is enterprise-grade over-engineering 
masquerading as sophistication. Your commands have more thinking blocks than a 
philosophy PhD dissertation and more complexity than NASA mission control.

The irony is palpable: commands preaching simplicity are themselves complex 
violations of every principle they claim to enforce.

RECOMMENDATION: Fire whoever approved this architecture and start over.
